description: Enable serde_json preserve_order feature so JSON output keys match model member order (matching AWS CLI output)

verify.expect: Keys in order: UserId, Account, Arn (not alphabetical)

notes: Session 8: Added preserve_order feature to serde_json. XML protocol parsers already iterated model members in order, so this was sufficient.

description: Normalize timestamps to AWS CLI format: ISO 8601 with +00:00 offset, no .000 milliseconds, epoch seconds converted to ISO strings
notes: Session 8: Added normalize_timestamp() and epoch_to_iso() in protocol/mod.rs. XML parsers normalize during parse. Driver calls normalize_response_value() for all protocols.

description: Strip pagination-related fields (output_token, more_results) from single-page auto-paginated responses to match AWS CLI

verify.expect: No IsTruncated or Marker fields in output

notes: Session 8: Fixed merge_pages to strip output_token and more_results fields even for single-page results.

description: JSON and REST-JSON protocol responses have keys reordered to match model member order via normalize_response_value()

verify.expect: Keys match AWS CLI output order

notes: Session 8: normalize_response_value() walks model shapes and reorders structure keys to match model member order, plus converts timestamps.

description: JSON protocol epoch timestamps (numbers) are converted to ISO 8601 strings matching AWS CLI format

verify.expect: Epoch numbers converted to ISO strings

notes: Session 8: normalize_response_value walks model shapes and converts timestamp numbers to ISO strings

description: Text output format (--output text) matches AWS CLI exactly for common operations

verify.run: diff <(cargo run -- sts get-caller-identity --output text 2>&1) <(aws sts get-caller-identity --output text 2>&1)

notes: Session 8: Verified text output matches exactly for STS get-caller-identity

description: --no-paginate flag bypasses auto-pagination and shows raw API response

verify.expect: Shows raw response including Truncated field

notes: Session 8: Already implemented in args.rs and driver.rs. Verified working.

description: Table output format (--output table) matches AWS CLI format (title row with operation name, proper column widths)

verify.run: diff <(cargo run -- sts get-caller-identity --output table 2>&1) <(aws sts get-caller-identity --output table 2>&1)

notes: Session 8: Table format differs - raws omits title row with operation name, column widths differ slightly
Session 9: Rewrote table formatter to match AWS CLI algorithm - proportional column scaling, alphabetical key sorting, AWS-style border rendering. STS get-caller-identity output now matches exactly.

description: Auto-paginated responses only include result_key and non_aggregate_keys fields (strip all other response fields like KeyCount)

verify.expect: Only Keys in output, no KeyCount or Truncated

notes: Session 9: Fixed by dropping non-model keys in normalize_response_value (matching botocore which only deserializes model members). KMS KeyCount was a non-model field being passed through. Pagination still keeps non-result-key model fields like S3 Owner.

description: Error messages for common failures match AWS CLI format and exit codes

verify.run: cargo run -- s3api get-object --bucket nonexistent-bucket --key test --region us-east-1 2>&1; echo $?
verify.expect: Error message and exit code match AWS CLI

notes: Session 9: Changed error format from 'AWS Error (HTTP N): code - msg' to 'An error occurred (code) when calling the Op operation: msg'. Exit code changed to 254 for service errors (was 1). Updated CliExitError variants to ServiceError(254)/ClientError(255).

description: Help text (--help) for services and operations is generated from model documentation
notes: Session 9: Already implemented - help text shows operation description and parameters from model. Not full man-page parity with AWS CLI but shows model documentation.

description: Handle empty lists and null values consistently with the AWS CLI output

verify.run: diff <(cargo run -- s3api list-buckets --region us-east-1 2>&1) <(aws s3api list-buckets --region us-east-1 2>&1)

notes: Session 9: Added fill_missing_top_level_members() to add null for missing output shape members (matching botocore). Re-strips pagination fields after fill. S3 list-buckets now matches exactly.
